gaps: A snapshot freshness field (last-upstream-refresh date) surfaced in record/context.json, so a predictor can tell a genuinely pending forward case from a stale-corpus one without risking an outcome-revealing live fetch
Statpack cut by event kind (evt-appeal-disposition vs cert petitions) and by originating district, not just by court
fedcourts query relevance for circuit appeals is weak: topic-filtered priors surfaced a mislabeled row, and there is no way to filter on appellate posture (appellant type, disposition-stage)
helpful: metrics/statpack.md per-court base rates (CA1 row drove the label distribution)
Reading src normalize_disposition/granted_flag to learn that affirmed/reversed both map to `other` — decisive for choosing the predicted label
fedcourts query --era as a sanity check that modern CA1 cases resolve overwhelmingly `other`
notes: Forward cell with six-years-stale docket data (see flags.json): the safest play was to skip CourtListener MCP entirely rather than risk retrieving the realized outcome, so the MCP surface went unused this run.
tools_used: repo source inspection (disposition normalizer, outcome projection)
